text: l awkward, upset, and uneasy. It is a secret I haven't revealed, but I will share with my subscribers the speaking characteristics of people who are charming even without designer bags, through the book and [Music]. Hello, my subscribers. I am Kim Ji-yoon, CEO of Seum Speech, a communication expert who has resolved the speaking anxiety of 2,000 people. [Music] Nice to meet you. While appearance changes with time, your way of speaking lasts a lifetime. If you cannot correct your way of speaking, you may end up lonely in your old age. Think about a conversation with someone you like. With some people, you feel incredibly happy after a conversation, while with others, you feel awkward, upset, and uneasy. As such, our way of speaking has a greater impact on our lives than we realize. According to Harvard research, a person's voice and tone determine their credibility and likeability. What kind of speaking style are you using? Let's check it together today. [Music] I will tell you about three speaking characteristics of people who lack confidence. The commonality is that they cannot express themselves. The first is, "Uh, they just talk like this." When speaking, "Ah, I..." But then, they say things like, "Ah, uh, that..." Everyone, the biggest problem is that the flow of speech gets interrupted. When the flow is interrupted, it becomes impossible to gauge what message you are trying to convey to the other person, leading to a frustrating situation. The second type is the one who overuses exclamations like "Wow!" or "Amazing!" Of course, exclamations can pop out when something truly joyful happens. It is fine once or twice, but if you only use exclamations without following them with your own thoughts or opinions, you can come across as childish. And finally, the third type is the one who trails off at the end of their sentences. When speaking, they might say, "I think I did this or that"—I call that "ant-like" speech. However, when sentences trail off like this, clarity is lost, making you appear passive. It is an unfortunate situation. I have seen a great many people come to me during my speech training because they have suffered disadvantages in social evaluations due to their speaking style. I think there are broadly two types. The first is that they become withdrawn because they cannot speak well. Their sentences trail off, and they tremble in certain situations. As a result, they end up avoiding those situations. title: 흥분하지 않고 우아하게 말하는 사람들은 꼭 '이런 말투'로 말한다 (김지윤 스피치 소통 전문가)
text: re are broadly two types. The first is that they become withdrawn because they cannot speak well. Their sentences trail off, and they tremble in certain situations. As a result, they end up avoiding those situations. Consequently, they lose the initiative in the conversation itself. You end up becoming passive. And secondly, you actually suffer disadvantages in social evaluations. Many people interviewing for promotions say things like this: "I should have emphasized to my significant other what I did like this, but I could n't say a word at all. I feel like I lived like a fool, having done nothing but work." You are right, everyone. Actual research has shown that a person's skills are evaluated lower due to an unstable tone of voice. Therefore, you must [music] master your tone, speaking habits, and speaking skills. I will explain three methods proven by over 2,000 people to speak without getting nervous. First, the first is to change the direction of your gaze. Try shifting your focus from yourself to the message. What I mean is, when tension suddenly rises during a presentation or conversation—thinking, " What if I fail?" or "I have to be perfect"—the more you lose control and your words get tangled. However, the idea is to shift your thinking from yourself, the object of your anxiety, to the keyword of delivery. If you shift your thoughts to things like, "How can I speak effectively?" or "How can I put the key keywords at the beginning?", your nervousness will drop significantly. And secondly, [with] your body... It is to remember. Everyone, you have probably all experienced this when riding a bicycle: you lose your balance, think "Oh, what should I do?", and then, if you've practiced a lot, suddenly zoom to your destination. Similarly, when speaking, sometimes the body reacts faster than the mind. This is called automatic thinking. For example, imagine you are at a presentation. You think, "I need to take the keyword on the left side of the slide with my eyes, and as I move to the next slide, I need to focus my eyes on the one in the center to speak." These actions become structured, allowing you to remember and your body to react. Therefore, rather than sitting still and practicing slowly, you must practice naturally while moving so that your body becomes motivated. Now, the third and final point is to speak as if you are having a conversation. Usually, when asked which is more comfortable, speaking or presenting, most people say,

title: 흥분하지 않고 우아하게 말하는 사람들은 꼭 '이런 말투'로 말한다 (김지윤 스피치 소통 전문가)
text: ust practice naturally while moving so that your body becomes motivated. Now, the third and final point is to speak as if you are having a conversation. Usually, when asked which is more comfortable, speaking or presenting, most people say, "I am more comfortable speaking." Why? Because there is a sense of continuity. People feel anxious when they feel disconnected. However, a presentation situation lacks that sense of continuity, and there is the pressure of having to get it done. Therefore, you can use this continuity to your advantage. When presenting, say things like, "Did you understand? Yes. May we move on to the next slide? I will take the next passage in a moment." By nodding your head to guide your breathing and initiate conversation, anxiety decreases, and confidence... [Music] It's getting higher. I'll tell you about the speaking characteristics of people who exude elegance even without a designer bag. The first is a soft and calm tone. Usually, many of us tend to put force into our voices, speak forcefully, or speak loudly. However, these elegant people use a middle-range pitch that is neither too exaggerated nor too quiet. In fact, according to research results, the most pleasant pitch range for men is 120 to 150 Hz, and for women, 200 to 220 Hz. So, I recommend that if your voice is too loud, try lowering it a little, and if it is too quiet, try raising the volume slightly. To speak elegantly, please avoid a high-pitched, exaggerated voice. A student came to see me; it was a truly unfortunate story by any standard. She was failing flight attendant interviews every time. However, her voice was like this: " Hello everyone." What do you think? Do you think this voice is kind? To talk about this, I would like to explain the concept of the Duchenne smile. Named after the French neurologist Guillaume Duchenne, a genuine smile is the Duchenne smile, meaning the orbicularis oculi, orbicularis major, and orbicularis oris muscles move simultaneously. Simply put, a genuine smile involves the corners of the eyes drooping and the corners of the mouth turning up. Speaking merely by raising your tone without this visual, genuine smile can actually appear artificial to the other person. Therefore, I hope you will break away from the notion that a "solo tone" implies unconditional kindness, and remember that only sincerity moves others. People with such charisma are good at separating the situation from the person. What I mean by this is

title: 흥분하지 않고 우아하게 말하는 사람들은 꼭 '이런 말투'로 말한다 (김지윤 스피치 소통 전문가)
text: ope you will break away from the notion that a "solo tone" implies unconditional kindness, and remember that only sincerity moves others. People with such charisma are good at separating the situation from the person. What I mean by this is that when we face a situation, we often start by making a judgment—saying, "Mr. Kim Cheol-su, that is all you can do." However, you must separate the situation from the person to become a truly dignified person. Let me give you an example: Mr. Kim Cheol-su, the deadline wasn't met; was there some kind of issue? This approach of objectively separating the situation from the person prevents the other person from becoming defensive and allows for a more constructive conversation to take place. This applies to everyday life as well. For instance, one might judge someone by saying, "You're bad at studying because you take after your dad." In reality, the child might have studied hard, or they might have put in their own efforts. Therefore, I hope you cultivate the habit of always speaking while separating the situation from the person. The people of Wuhan The point is that vocalization does not rely on the vocal cords. When viewed through an endoscope, the vocal cords are made of muscle tissue that is thinner than you might think. You have experienced tiny water droplets echoing when you go into a cave, right? There are spaces in our faces that act like such caves. Try looking for them. Where are the empty spaces above our vocal cords? There are the paranasal cavity, the nasal cavity, and the pharyngeal cavity. When these empty spaces amplify sound, it becomes the "half air, half sound" sound—the phrase popularized by Park Jin-young. So, when you produce sound, do not hold it against your vocal cords and speak in a straight line; instead, get into the habit of speaking in a curve. Imagine a hard candy not resting on your molars, and think of the sound flowing down the hard palate and the roof of your mouth. If you speak with this mindset, you will be able to produce a much softer voice. [Music] I think there are two differences between people who are skilled and those whose skills are recognized. The first is the ability to grasp intentions. A boss asked, " How was the meeting?" and you replied, "Oh, I went to a client, and that manager got really angry at me." text: e meeting?" and you replied, "Oh, I went to a client, and that manager got really angry at me." Why did your boss ask this question? They likely wanted to hear the results of the meeting. However, T1 is expressing emotion. That is why he is considered to be good at his job. Recognized individuals shared a common trait: an exceptional ability to grasp intentions. The second characteristic is clear communication skills. Because they focus on the quality of their words rather than the quantity when speaking, they prioritize the purpose. Therefore, being clear actually means speaking accurately and without unnecessary details. You have likely experienced situations where the more you try to speak or be specific, the more you stray off course. That is why people who speak only to the essentials and without embellishments are the ones who excel at their jobs and are recognized for it. I will now explain four characteristics of professionals. The first is speaking one thing at a time. Let's assume you are explaining how to use a kiosk to someone using it for the first time. Try this right now. Some people might say something like this: " There is a home button. You see the 'Back' button next to it, right? But if you look to the left of that 'Back' button, you see a menu section. If you scroll down within that menu section, you will see various functions. Oh my, this is too difficult." But they might say, "I will explain it one thing at a time. You see the menu, right? Please select a menu. And please select a payment method. Third, once you complete the payment, you are done." It is much easier to understand, isn't it? That is why one One characteristic of an ace employee is conveying only one message per sentence. Second, you must speak using numbering. Since we often write in report format, we are accustomed to using bullet points and numbers; however, it seems you don't often think about numbering when speaking. But, if we speak by breaking it down into "first," "second," and " third," the advantage is that there is no duplication or omission. It is a secret I haven't revealed, but I will share it with you, my three subscribers, through this book. Hold up your five fingers. Okay, the first is the argument. Evidence one, two, three. The last is the summary. So, you are emphasizing it once again. For example, let's say you recommend hiking as a hobby. title: 흥분하지 않고 우아하게 말하는 사람들은 꼭 '이런 말투'로 말한다 (김지윤 스피치 소통 전문가)
text: nt. Evidence one, two, three. The last is the summary. So, you are emphasizing it once again. For example, let's say you recommend hiking as a hobby. Since you are suddenly asked to speak, you might say, " Hiking isn't my hobby, so how am I supposed to talk about it?" But you should give it a try. You should say, " Try hiking as a hobby. First of all, it improves your health. Second, it relieves stress. Third, it even improves social relationships. Therefore, I highly recommend hiking for its physical, social, and psychological support." It's not difficult, right? Argument, Evidence one, two, three, and then summary. Please make sure to practice this at home. Third, speaking specifically. It became a hot topic on social media for a while: "Two friends went out for drinks and decided to split the bill." The bill came to 60,000 won. One friend said, "I'll pay an extra 10,000 won," so I assumed, "I'll pay 40,000 won, so that friend will pay 20,000 won." However, another friend paid 35,000 won and the other paid 25,000 won. What do you think? If we went back to this situation and had said specifically, "I'll pay 40,000 won, and you pay 20,000 won," there really wouldn't have been any room for misunderstanding. Therefore, to clearly reduce the possibility of misunderstanding, please remember just this: avoid "brother-in-law" (hyungbu). To explain why, avoid adjectives and adverbs, and use nouns, verbs, and numerals. Everyone has different standards for adjectives and adverbs. If you say, "It's very good," or "It's so exciting," people might wonder, "How good is that? What is the standard for 'very good'?" However, when explaining projects, say, "Project A is scheduled to be completed within 30 days." Now, regarding " Project A," we used the precise noun, accurately described the action of completion, and mentioned the number of months, which helps reduce the possibility of misunderstanding. So, everyone, stay away from "brother-in-law." If you know this speaking style, you will be able to speak well. It is called the " Hamburger Speech." If you look at a hamburger, there is a bun and various fillings inside. However, no matter how abundant the fillings are, if even one bun is missing, the contents collapse. We refer to this as a lack of logic. In other words, if your opening and closing statements are not properly concluded, the other person may not understand what you are saying. Therefore, the Hamburger Speech is called that because its

title: 흥분하지 않고 우아하게 말하는 사람들은 꼭 '이런 말투'로 말한다 (김지윤 스피치 소통 전문가)
text: pse. We refer to this as a lack of logic. In other words, if your opening and closing statements are not properly concluded, the other person may not understand what you are saying. Therefore, the Hamburger Speech is called that because its purpose is to conclude by following the structure of claim, evidence, one, two, three, and then the claim again. But just as the meat in a hamburger isn't repeated—meat is followed by vegetables and then tomatoes— if you organize your evidence in a diverse way, the other person will perceive you as a logical person. Let's take an example. Suppose you are stating the claim that we need to hold a meeting. Then, you would say, "Evidence 1: To generate ideas, to share progress, to check if completion is proceeding well. Therefore, a meeting is necessary." Instead of simply saying, "Have a meeting," if you state the claim and evidence in the order of one, two, and then say, "That is why I believe we need to hold a meeting," you will appear much more logical. But here is a great tip: when making your final claim, [something] Rather than saying "it seems," please conclude with a definitive predicate like "it is Momo." It will appear much more logical. Having met about 2,000 people, I encountered diverse professional groups. I met chairmen and wealthy individuals. However, there was a common characteristic among them—specifically, a trait shared by successful people—which was that they manage themselves well. Shall I be more specific? They manage their impressions well. This does not refer to having a pretty or handsome face. Do you know the meaning of the word "face"? It refers to the form drawn by one's mind. In other words, it is a shape that reflects one's emotions and thoughts. That is why I have witnessed many times that these people strive to maintain their energy in a balanced state by focusing not only on their outward appearance but also on their inner state. So, if you want to speak well, why not try listening to the voice within yourself first? [Music]
